@retry.description: Imperative verb on a button/action that re-attempts a failed operation. Distinct from errors.action.retryFailed (the toast shown when the retry itself fails).

@actionFailed.description: Generic fallback TITLE for an error toast when a UI action fails and no more specific errors.action.* title applies. Subject is left unstated on purpose - keep it short and generic.

@close.description: Verb meaning dismiss / shut (a panel, drawer, dialog), NOT the adjective 'near'. Used as an aria-label on the mobile-nav backdrop and other close affordances - translate as the imperative verb.

@commandBar.description: Placeholder text in the sidebar command-palette launcher (Cmd/Ctrl-K). 'command' = an in-app action you can search and trigger, not a shell command. Keep the trailing ellipsis.

@create.description: Sidebar SECTION HEADING grouping creation actions (e.g. Build a pipeline), not a button. Translate as the section label, which may differ grammatically from the 'Create' verb.

@bootstrapRepo.description: Action that scaffolds/initialises a brand-new empty git repository from a reference architecture. 'Bootstrap' here is the product's term for first-time repo creation.

@sandbox.description: Named feature area: an off-to-the-side screen for trying prompt versions / models against graded fixtures. A product feature name, not a generic 'sandbox environment'.

@kaizen.description: Proper-noun product feature name (grading history + verified prompt/agent/model combos). Do NOT translate - keep 'Kaizen' verbatim in every locale.

@workspaceContext.description: Sidebar section heading for the workspace's reusable prompt/context material (the fragment library). 'Context' = material fed to the AI agents, not generic UI context.

@contextFragments.description: Reusable, versioned best-practice prompt snippets that get folded into agent prompts. A domain term - 'fragments' are prompt building blocks, not file fragments.

@addService.description: Adds a 'service' to the board. A service is a top-level board frame (a project/codebase), the product's core unit - a domain term, translate consistently with how 'service' is rendered elsewhere.

@decisionWord.description: Count-based plural noun rendered AFTER a number, e.g. '3 decisions' (pending human decisions in the run queue). Resolved via t(key, count); count is always 1 or more. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - and rely on the custom pluralRules wired in i18n.config.ts).

@retryFailed.description: Error-toast title shown when RE-RUNNING a failed pipeline run (or agent run) itself fails.

@startFailed.description: Error-toast title when starting a pipeline run fails. Implied subject is the run/pipeline.

@mergeFailed.description: Error-toast title when merging the run's GitHub pull request fails. 'merge' = git/PR merge.

@restartFailed.description: Error-toast title when restarting a pipeline run / step fails.

@dependencies_unmet.description: Conflict-toast title: the task can't start because one or more tasks it depends on are not finished yet.

@task_limit_reached.description: Conflict-toast title: too many tasks are already running concurrently under this service (the workspace's task-limit setting). 'Concurrency' = number running at once.

@tester_infra_unsupported.description: Conflict-toast title for the Tester step's start gate. UMBRELLA over two cases: (a) the service has no test infra configured, AND (b) this deployment's container runtime can't run local test infra (limited mode). The detailed description carries the specific cause; keep the title broad ('not configured / unavailable').

@agent_backend_unconfigured.description: Conflict-toast title: no execution backend (Cloudflare container / self-hosted runner pool) is wired to run container agent steps.

@run_not_retryable.description: Conflict-toast title. 'Run' is a NOUN here = a pipeline execution (only a failed run can be retried). Translate 'Run' as the execution noun, not the verb 'to run'.

@no_pr_to_merge.description: Conflict-toast title. 'PR' = GitHub pull request; keep the abbreviation if it is the established term in your locale, else expand it.

@bootstrap_not_retryable.description: Conflict-toast title. 'Bootstrap' = a repo-bootstrap job (see nav.bootstrapRepo); only a failed bootstrap job can be retried.

@bootstrap_reference_missing.description: Conflict-toast title. 'Reference architecture' is a domain term: a saved template repository a bootstrap was based on. 'is gone' = it was since deleted, so the bootstrap can't be retried.

conflict.fallbackMessage: This action conflicts with the current state.

@fallbackMessage.description: Generic DESCRIPTION (toast body) used for a 409 conflict that carries no human-readable server message. Shown under whichever conflict title applies.

providersUnconfigured.title: No AI provider for this model
providersUnconfigured.body: No provider is configured for {models}. Add a provider key, connect a subscription, or enable Cloudflare AI to run it.

@body.description: Toast body when a pipeline step's model has no usable AI provider. Keep the named placeholder for the model list intact (a comma-separated list of model ids is injected). Offers the same remedy as the startup 'no AI model' banner.

@action.description: Label on the toast's action button that jumps to the AI-provider setup screen.
